subject: Anthropology
title: Social Organization
description: A quick win: understand Social Organization.

hook.text: Some societies have kings. Others have elders. A few have no leaders at all. All of them work.

buildup.text: Social organization is how a society structures relationships — through kinship, rank, gender, age, and class.

discovery.text: Band societies are egalitarian and small. Chiefdoms have ranked lineages. States have formal governments and law.

twist.text: No form is natural or inevitable. Each reflects specific ecological, historical, and cultural conditions.

climax.text: Studying social organization reveals that power and equality are human choices, not fixed laws of nature.

punchline.text: How we organize is always a choice.

quiz.question: What does social organization refer to?
options: How a society structures relationships through kinship, rank, and class
